title: Yelp Unifies ML Model Training with Training Orchestrator
gist: Yelp replaced per-team Spark training scripts with a configuration-driven, DAG-based Training Orchestrator to centralize ML pipeline orchestration, reflecting an industry-wide move (Netflix Metaflow, Uber Michelangelo) toward unified training infrastructure as model and team counts grow.

insights.claim: Yelp's per-team Spark training scripts produced duplicated code, inconsistent configurations, and fragile custom monitoring, a recurring failure mode in large multi-team ML platforms.
insights.speaker: Claudio Masolo
insights.so_what: Ad hoc orchestration becomes a bottleneck at scale; centralized ML platform tooling is a persistent enterprise need worth watching for vendors.
insights.quote: This led to duplicated code, inconsistent configurations, and fragile custom monitoring.

insights.claim: The same fragmentation problem Yelp solved internally drove Netflix's Metaflow (recently adding a configuration object) and Uber's Michelangelo, indicating convergent architecture choices across major engineering orgs.
insights.speaker: Claudio Masolo
insights.so_what: Convergence toward unified training platforms validates the category; commercial MLOps orchestration vendors have a clear enterprise pull.
insights.quote: Many companies with several ML teams are moving towards a centralised, clear training infrastructure.
insights.locator: Yelp's effort reflects a wider trend

insights.claim: Training Orchestrator separates 'what to run' from 'how it runs' using Pydantic-validated configuration objects that build a DAG executed in topological order, letting identical step definitions run locally, in Jupyter, or in production unchanged.
insights.speaker: Claudio Masolo
insights.so_what: Decoupling training logic from cluster runtime enables local testing and faster iteration, the core value prop of modern ML orchestration tools.
insights.quote: Training Orchestrator separates what to run from how it runs.
insights.locator: Training Orchestrator separates what to run

insights.claim: Schema validation catches configuration mismatches (bad step input/output pairings, out-of-range parameters) in seconds before any compute is spent, versus hours into a running Spark job under the old approach.
insights.speaker: Claudio Masolo
insights.so_what: Fail-fast validation cuts wasted cloud compute, a concrete cost lever for ML-heavy orgs.
insights.quote: This is much faster than finding them hours into a running Spark job.
insights.locator: Schema validation quickly catches configuration mismatches

insights.claim: Every run's complete configuration is logged automatically as an MLflow artifact, making runs replayable from a single record and solving the prior provenance and reproducibility gaps.
insights.speaker: Claudio Masolo
insights.so_what: Automatic provenance is a governance/reproducibility feature increasingly demanded in regulated ML.
insights.locator: Every run's complete configuration is logged automatically

insights.claim: Yelp frames the upfront cost of schema and step-type design plus migrating existing scripts as centralizing a cost teams would otherwise pay repeatedly, an explicit build-once amortization argument.
insights.speaker: Claudio Masolo
insights.so_what: The amortization framing is how platform teams justify internal-tooling investment; useful lens for evaluating build-vs-buy MLOps decisions.
insights.quote: Yelp views this as a way to centralise the cost, so teams don't have to pay it repeatedly.
insights.locator: The tradeoff involves an initial investment

insights.claim: Training Orchestrator is an internal framework and not an open-source release, with planned additions for model evaluation, comparison, explanation, and orchestration-layer lineage tracking.
insights.speaker: Claudio Masolo
insights.so_what: Kept internal, so no direct OSS competitive threat; the roadmap signals what enterprise ML platforms will demand next.
insights.quote: It is an internal framework, not an open-source release.
insights.locator: The Training Orchestrator works with Yelp's ML platform
